creator: nigel@netmsi.com
text:
Kernel panic during a php install today with 

panic: ffs_valloc: dup alloc

The machine rebooted and could initialize the root partition but quickly panicked again (immedately after a boot message - something about clearing /tmp).

Rebooted in single user mode and ran fsck with journal.  Rebooted and was immediately greeted by the same panic.

Rebooted in single user mode and ran fsck WITHOUT journal.  Various UFS corruptions involving incorrect superblock data and unreferenced files owned by root were repaired and I rebooted to a working system.
